summary.name: Momentum Effect Combined with Term Structure in Commodities
summary.author: Jing Wu
summary.description: The strategy is a cross sectional commodity Futures model that seeks to harvest two well documented premia: term structure carry and time series momentum. Each month it measures each commodity's implied annualized roll return by comparing the nearest contract price to deferred contracts within roughly the next 90 days, favoring markets in backwardation (positive carry) and avoiding or shorting those in contango (negative carry). It then ranks commodities by carry, forms top and bottom carry
summary.description_full:
The strategy is a cross sectional commodity Futures model that seeks to harvest two well documented premia: term structure carry and time series momentum. Each month it measures each commodity's implied annualized roll return by comparing the nearest contract price to deferred contracts within roughly the next 90 days, favoring markets in backwardation (positive carry) and avoiding or shorting those in contango (negative carry). It then ranks commodities by carry, forms top and bottom carry buckets, and within each bucket applies a momentum filter based on the 21 day average of daily returns, selecting the stronger half of the positive carry group for long positions and the weaker half of the negative carry group for short positions.

The investable universe is a diversified set of liquid commodity Futures across energies, metals, grains, softs, and meats. Positions are set at month start, equally weighted within the selected long and short sets with small fixed gross exposure (about 5 percent long and 5 percent short, scaled by the number of signals). Risk is primarily controlled through diversification, balanced long short construction, and systematic rebalancing as contracts and signals evolve, with implicit contract selection relying on sufficient chain depth and liquidity proxies (e.g., open interest and valid prices).
